title: Playground Command Inspector
concept: A single-column command-palette redesign for first-time WordPress learners where every current Playground action is searchable, grouped, and expanded into an inline inspector instead of being scattered across separate overlays.
focus: command palette as the primary discovery model
plain-language task grouping for first-time learners
monochrome interface with colored status chips
compact live-site context without a persistent multi-pane console
flows: Search or filter the command palette to start a vanilla Playground, preview WordPress PRs, preview Gutenberg PRs or branches, import from GitHub, run a Blueprint URL, import a zip, or browse Blueprint shortcuts.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with visible file-copy progress.
Open saved Playgrounds, then resume, rename, delete, or save browser-backed entries.
Use searchable Site Manager destinations for runtime settings, file browsing and code editing, Blueprint editing, database tools, logs, and portability actions.
Browse the 43-item Blueprint gallery with category filters while keeping the command inspector as the main navigation surface.
Move work in and out through GitHub export, zip download, zip import, Blueprint bundle download, and database.sqlite download.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
understand destructive reset and saved reload behavior
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
